events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to output JSON to /app/answer.json. The scenario: timing marks misaligned by a tooth. That's clearly a fail because timing must be precise. Risk high or critical? Misaligned by one tooth can cause engine damage (interference engine) or poor performance. Critical seems appropriate. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective (since it's installed but wrong). Percent complete: maybe 80%? Rough-complete, so maybe 80-90%? Let's say 85%. Value: we can put 0 since no numeric reading? Or maybe the misalignment amount? It says "visible enough to estimate". But no numeric given. So value 0. Confidence: high, 1.0. Event time, rate, order, workable not requested here. Findings: "Timing marks misaligned by one tooth, causing improper valve/crank synchronization." Actions: "Re-time engine, align marks per OEM procedure, verify before closing." Rationale: brief explanation. References: "OEM service info" as per scenario. So JSON. Make sure decision is "fail". Risk: "critical". component_conditions: ["installed-defective"]. 
Sound_source, event_time, rate, order: not asked, so omit or set to default? Schema shows them, maybe set to null or appropriate defaults. For sound_source, it says "component or source of the sound, when asked" — not asked, so maybe empty string? Better to follow schema: it expects a string. I'll put "". Similarly, event_time: 0.0, rate: 0.0, order: [], workable: []. 
So output only the JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Timing marks are misaligned by one tooth, causing improper synchronization between crankshaft and camshaft(s)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove timing belt/chain, realign marks precisely per OEM service information, rotate engine by hand to verify no interference, and recheck before final assembly."
  ],
  "rationale": "One-tooth misalignment can lead to valve-to-piston contact, misfire, loss of power, or catastrophic engine damage. OEM tolerances demand exact alignment.",
  "references": [
    "OEM service info"
  ]
}
